name: Rule configuration list for a promotion
description: GET response (Template List Configuration). Returns the `rules` array, each item a Template Configuration with documented fields: event, journalSubType, journalSubTypeName, journalType, priority, reward, ruleName, templateName. No top-level JSON example is given in the doc, so this fixture is built strictly from the Template List Configuration + Template Configuration property tables.
notes: Method documented as both GET and POST on the same resource; this entry models the GET (read) per the target. requestExample is null because GET has no body. The doc's "Resource" line writes the path variable literally as `promotionId` (no braces); I normalized it to `{promotionId}` to mark it as a placeholder. CAVEAT: the doc provides NO concrete JSON example for the GET "Template List Configuration" response, only property tables for the wrapper (single field `rules: Template Configuration[]`, line 33078) and the nested Template Configuration (lines 33049-33069). The `event` and `reward` fields are typed `List<Map<String,Object>>` (free-form maps), so their inner keys (operator/field/value, rewardType/pointType) are IMPLEMENTER-DEFINED illustrative values, NOT documented field names, only the top-level keys event/journalSubType/journalSubTypeName/journalType/priority/reward/ruleName/templateName are documented. journalSubTypeName available from 64.0; all other fields from 60.0 (resource Available version = 60.0). No error/empty example is shown in the doc, so only a success fixture was created. For POST (create), the request body property table is at lines 23854-23896 (adds promotionLimit, Optional, 63.0) and the POST response body is "Unified Promotion Templates" (line 33564) which the doc states "varies based on the template selected" with no schema/example.
